page: Microsoft Azure DC2ds_v3 is a virtual machine belonging to the DDCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AVX-512 VNNI, AES, and VAES. The server is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates average performance in single-core workloads but shows limitations in multi-core and memory bandwidth tests. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is average at 1897.09, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1664.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3764.07 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3005.0. Memory bandwidth is also weak, measuring 26.16 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 19.57 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

From a resource density perspective, this configuration offers a qualitative balance for memory-optimized workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. The dedicated physical cores eliminate hyperthreading resource sharing, though the overall multi-core throughput remains low. It is suitable for lightweight database operations, basic text processing, and small-scale language model inference, where its 16.0 GB memory capacity and local storage can be leveraged without demanding high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded compute.
description: Microsoft Azure DC2ds_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine in the DDCSv3 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1664.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1897.09. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 3005.0 and large-block read bandwidth of 26.16 GB/sec. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-optimized workloads that do not require high parallel processing, such as small database operations, basic text processing, and lightweight single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC2ds_v3 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel cores and local storage for single-threaded and lightweight database workloads.

16.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
154 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance for single-threaded tasks
Suited for lightweight databases, text processing, and small-scale inference
